# Keith Dow

Keith Dow, PhD, is a Canadian theologian, researcher, writer, and accessibility consultant working at the intersection of Christian theology, disability, intellectual and developmental disability, care ethics, spirituality, and belonging.

Primary site: https://keithdow.com
Substack: https://keithdow.substack.com/

## Key pages

- About: https://keithdow.com/about
- Writing & Work: https://keithdow.com/writing-and-work
- Formed Together: https://keithdow.com/formed-together
- Images of God: https://keithdow.com/images-of-god

## Major works

- Formed Together: Mystery, Narrative, and Virtue in Christian Caregiving. Baylor University Press, 2021.
- "Against Living Saints" in The Betrayal of Witness: Reflections on the Downfall of Jean Vanier. Wipf and Stock, 2024.
- Thousands of Glittering Shards: Spirituality as Resonance in the Lives of People with Intellectual Disabilities. Religions, 2023.
- Toward Accessible Faith & Flourishing: Reconsidering Greek Intellectualism in Western Christian Theology. Journal of Disability and Religion, 2021.

## Research project

Images of God: Through the Lens of Disability explores how Christian adults with intellectual disabilities understand God and themselves through arts-based research.

Project site: https://imagesofgod.ca

## Topics

Disability theology, intellectual disability, developmental disability, Christian caregiving, belonging, accessibility, practical theology, Kierkegaard, ethics of care, spirituality, Christian community, disability ministry.
